entries.definition:
-ي q–s–w/y hardness, harshness, severity; to be solid, to be hard, to be cruel, to suffer, to be harsh. Of this root, three forms occur seven times in the Qur’an: قَسَت qasat three times; قَاسِيَة qāsiyatun three times and قَسْوَة qaswatun once.
قَسَا qasā ū [v. intrans.] to be, or become hard, or cruel (2:74) ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِيَ كَالْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً even after that, your hearts became hard, as hard as rocks, or even harder.

قَاسِيَة qāsiyatun [quasi-fem. act. part.] cruel, hard (5:13) فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِمْ مِيثَاقَهُمْ لَعَنَّاهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَا قُلُوبَهُمْ قَاسِيَةً on account of their breaking their pledge, We cursed them and hardened their hearts.

قَسْوَة qaswatun [n.] hardness, harshness, cruelty (2:74) ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِيَ كَالْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً even after that, your hearts became hard, as hard as rocks, or even harder.
